openmeter/ingest/kafkaingest.purpose: Implements the ingest.Collector interface by producing CloudEvents to per-namespace Kafka topics using confluent-kafka-go. Owns topic resolution and provisioning on every Ingest call, OTel span lifecycle per event, Kafka header construction (namespace, specversion, ingested_at, OTel span context), and the KafkaProducerGroup oklog/run pair for async delivery reporting.

patterns.name: Serializer injection for key and value encoding
patterns.description: Collector never encodes events directly — it delegates to serializer.Serializer. SerializeKey encodes a dedupe.Item (namespace+source+id) as JSON for sink-worker deduplication; SerializeValue encodes a flat CloudEventsKafkaPayload. Both must be called in order inside every Ingest invocation.
patterns.example:
key, _ := s.Serializer.SerializeKey(topicName, namespace, ev)
value, _ := s.Serializer.SerializeValue(topicName, ev)

patterns.name: Topic resolved and provisioned inside every Ingest call
patterns.description: TopicResolver.Resolve maps namespace → topic name; TopicProvisioner.Provision ensures the topic exists before Produce is called. Both happen per call, not at construction time. The provisioner is expected to be idempotent (no-op on existing topics).
patterns.example:
topicName, err := s.TopicResolver.Resolve(ctx, namespace)
_ = s.TopicProvisioner.Provision(ctx, pkgkafka.TopicConfig{Name: topicName, Partitions: s.TopicPartitions})

patterns.name: OTel span wraps full Ingest lifecycle with deferred error recording
patterns.description: Ingest opens a span at entry and captures the local err variable in a deferred closure that calls span.RecordError + span.SetStatus on any non-nil error. Never return an error path without this span capturing it.

patterns.name: Fixed Kafka header set per message
patterns.description: Every kafka.Message must include four headers: 'namespace' ([]byte), 'specversion', 'ingested_at' (RFC3339Nano UTC via ToIngestedAt), and otelx.OTelSpanContextKey (serialized span context). The sink worker reads these headers to reconstruct context and attribute events to the correct tenant.

patterns.name: KafkaProducerGroup as oklog/run pair, not an internal goroutine
patterns.description: KafkaProducerGroup returns (execute func() error, interrupt func(error)) for use in cmd/* oklog/run.Group. The execute loop drains producer.Events() handling *kafka.Message delivery reports, *kafka.Stats (metrics), and kafka.Error (broker errors). Must be started by the caller — never start the producer without running this group.
patterns.example:
execute, interrupt := kafkaingest.KafkaProducerGroup(ctx, producer, logger, kafkaMetrics)
runGroup.Add(execute, interrupt)

patterns.name: NamespaceHandler reuses same Resolver and Provisioner as Collector
patterns.description: NamespaceHandler.CreateNamespace calls Resolve then Provision; DeleteNamespace is a no-op unless DeletionEnabled=true. The TopicResolver nil check is done at call time, not construction, so a zero-value NamespaceHandler will panic in production.

key_file_guides.role: Core Collector struct with Ingest (produce path) and Close methods, plus KafkaProducerGroup for async delivery reporting. All Kafka produce logic lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Producer.Produce is fire-and-forget; delivery results arrive asynchronously via KafkaProducerGroup. Missing the group means silent delivery failures. The local 'err' variable is captured by the deferred span closure — do not shadow it with := inside the function body.

key_file_guides.role: NamespaceHandler implements openmeter/namespace.Handler for Kafka ingest topics. Provisions on CreateNamespace; deprovisions only when DeletionEnabled=true.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TopicResolver nil-check is deferred to call time — a zero-value NamespaceHandler panics at runtime, not setup. Register this handler before initNamespace() in cmd/server/main.go or the default namespace misses provisioning.

key_file_guides.role: JSONSerializer implements SerializeKey (dedupe.Item JSON → key bytes) and SerializeValue (CloudEventsKafkaPayload JSON → value bytes). Wire format consumed by sink worker's FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CloudEventsKafkaPayload.Time is unix int64 — timezone is intentionally dropped. Data is stored as a JSON string inside the payload, not raw bytes. Do not change this without updating the sink worker's deserializer.

key_file_guides.role: Serializer interface definition with GetKeySchemaId/GetValueSchemaId schema-registry hooks. Current JSON impl returns 0 for both schema IDs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If adding a schema-registry-aware serializer, all four methods must return real schema IDs or the sink worker will mis-deserialize messages.

key_file_guides.role: NamespacedTopicResolver maps namespace → Kafka topic name via fmt.Sprintf(template, namespace). Default template produces 'om_%s_events'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Constructor validates non-empty template but not %s count. A template with zero or two %s placeholders silently produces wrong topic names.

key_file_guides.role: Resolver interface. Accepts context.Context for future async/DB-backed implementations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always propagate caller's ctx to Resolve — never pass context.Background() in application code.

anti_patterns: Calling Producer.Produce without running KafkaProducerGroup in an oklog/run.Group — delivery errors are silently dropped
Hard-coding topic names instead of calling TopicResolver.Resolve per namespace
Adding new Kafka headers inside Ingest without also updating the sink worker's header parsing
Constructing Collector or NamespaceHandler with a nil TopicResolver — nil check is deferred to call time and will panic in production
Storing timezone-aware time in CloudEventsKafkaPayload.Time — the field is unix int64 and timezone is intentionally stripped by the serializer

decisions.decision: Topic is resolved and provisioned on every Ingest call, not only at namespace creation
decisions.rationale: Ensures idempotent topic existence even when the NamespaceHandler was not called first (e.g., in self-hosted single-namespace deployments). The provisioner is expected to be idempotent (no-op on existing topics) so the per-call overhead is a single Kafka admin API check.

decisions.decision: Key encodes dedupe.Item (namespace+source+id) rather than event ID alone
decisions.rationale: Enables consistent Kafka partitioning per logical event identity so the sink worker's Redis deduplication hash matches the Kafka message key without a separate lookup, preventing double-counting on consumer restart.

decisions.decision: KafkaProducerGroup is an oklog/run pair returned to the caller rather than a goroutine started inside NewCollector
decisions.rationale: Lets cmd/* binaries control the delivery-reporting lifecycle via the shared run.Group, ensuring graceful shutdown and avoiding goroutine leaks — consistent with the oklog/run pattern used throughout all cmd/* entrypoints.

code_examples.scenario: Wiring Collector and registering NamespaceHandler in app/common
